briefing.incidentSummary:
A local restaurant received 47 five-star reviews in a single night — all from accounts created that same day.

A rival restaurant owner is suspected of running a fake review campaign to boost their own ratings while filing false complaints to pull down the victim's listing.

The accounts all trace back to the same device.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Identify who created the fake accounts

› Link the accounts to a single device

› Collect at least 3 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Suresh K
suspects.profileNotes: All 47 fake accounts were created from IP 103.24.56.88 registered to Spice Garden Restaurant's broadband connection. Device fingerprint matches Suresh K's tablet.

suspects.name: Meera J
suspects.profileNotes: Left a single negative review from her personal account. Verified human account, not part of the fake campaign.

suspects.name: Unknown Agency
suspects.profileNotes: No consistent device fingerprint. Accounts show different browser agents unlike the fake campaign which had identical fingerprints.

items.label: fake_accounts_creation_log.csv
items.detail: Account creation log from Foodie5Star platform's backend database. 47 user accounts were registered between 23:02 and 01:58 AM over a single night — all originating from IP 103.24.56.88. Each account was created with a unique email address following the pattern: random-word + 4-digit number @gmail or @yahoo. All 47 accounts bypassed the standard email verification step — logs show the verification token was generated and consumed within 200ms of issuance, consistent with an automated script hitting the verification endpoint directly. Post-creation, each account immediately posted a 5-star review for 'Spice Garden' restaurant before going dormant.

items.label: device_fingerprint_report.txt
items.detail: Device fingerprint analysis from the platform's fraud detection system. All 47 accounts share an identical browser fingerprint: User-Agent string 'Mozilla/5.0 (Android 13; SM-G991B) Chrome/118.0.0.0', screen resolution 412×915, timezone Asia/Kolkata, canvas fingerprint hash 7f3a9c21. This is statistically impossible for organic users — the probability of 47 different humans sharing the same canvas hash is less than 1 in 10 billion. The fingerprint matches a headless Chrome instance running an Android emulator. The script also failed to randomise the WebGL renderer string, which reads 'ANGLE (Google, Vulkan 1.1.0 on NVIDIA GeForce RTX series, OpenGL ES 3.2)'.

hiddenItem.label: ip_registration_record.txt
hiddenItem.detail: WHOIS and ISP registration data for IP address 103.24.56.88. The IP is assigned to a residential broadband account registered under the business name 'Spice Garden Hospitality Pvt Ltd' — the owner entity of Spice Garden restaurant. The account holder's name on the ISP record is Suresh K, matching the restaurant's registered proprietor. The account address is the same as the restaurant's registered address. This IP has not previously appeared in any fraud database. The registration predates the fake review campaign by 14 months, confirming this is the operator's regular internet connection — not a VPN or proxy.

minigame.title: Decode ISP Record
minigame.hint: Caesar shift -3. What business name is this?
hints: It is a restaurant name — two words.

onSuccess.message: ip_registration_record.txt is now available in your file feed.

items.label: Account Creation Timing
items.detail: Accounts were created in automated bursts — one every 3.6 minutes on average. This is consistent with a script or manual tab-switching, not organic user behavior.

items.label: IP Geolocation
items.detail: IP 103.24.56.88 geolocates to the commercial district of Koramangala — same block as Spice Garden Restaurant.

items.label: BiryaniHub Review Pattern
items.detail: A separate automated check of BiryaniHub's reviews was triggered by an anonymous tip suggesting they also ran fake review campaigns. Analysis shows BiryaniHub received 12 reviews over the same period — spread across 9 different IP addresses, 6 different device types, over 4 days. This is an organic distribution pattern. No IP clustering, no device fingerprint match, no account batch creation. The anonymous tip appears to be a misdirection attempt, possibly by the actual fraudster.
items.irrelevantReason: BiryaniHub's review pattern is organic. The anonymous tip was a misdirection. All fake review activity traces to 103.24.56.88 — Spice Garden's IP.

items.label: Single Origin IP
items.detail: All 47 accounts originate from one IP — 103.24.56.88. No proxy or VPN masking. The attacker made no attempt to hide their origin.

timeline.title: Victim Notices
timeline.description: Restaurant owner sees suspicious review spike

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Suresh K exposed as the fake review fraudster.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: The real culprit was Suresh K.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

aria.welcomeToHub:
...47 glowing five-star reviews. All fake. All from one night.
Someone wanted to win badly enough to cheat.
Find them.
aria.exploreFeeds:
Device fingerprints. IP logs. Account metadata.
The trail is wider than you think.
aria.viewEvidence: One IP. 47 accounts. That's not a coincidence.
aria.decryptionHint:
The business name is hidden in the ISP record.
Decode it.
aria.flagSuspect:
A trace is forming.
You're on to something — keep pulling the thread.
More fragments remain. Don't stop now.
